title: The Orchid Thief of Calcutta
author: Barış Kısır
description: In 1870s India, British botanist James Whitby and Bengali guide Priya Mukherjee race through the jungles of Assam to find a mythical orchid before a ruthless French collector can claim it. A story of obsession, trust, and the true cost of possession.
long_description: Calcutta, 1875. British botanist James Whitby hears whispers of a legendary flower hidden deep in the Assam jungles — the Fire Orchid, said to bloom once every seven years with petals like living embers. The Royal Horticultural Society funds his expedition half-heartedly, expecting failure. But James hires Priya Mukherjee, a brilliant Bengali naturalist who knows the hills better than any European and carries her own secrets: her husband died searching for the same flower, and she has seen it with her own eyes. Racing against Marcel Dubois — the Collector — a French aristocrat with no scruples and unlimited resources, James and Priya navigate fever-haunted jungles, treacherous cliffs, and their own growing trust. The orchid is not merely a prize. It is a test of what they are willing to sacrifice.

characters.James Whitby: British botanist, Royal Horticultural Society fellow. Methodical, earnest, carrying colonial assumptions. Yearns for a career-defining discovery.
characters.Priya Mukherjee: Bengali naturalist and guide. Widow of botanist Anjan Mukherjee. Sharp-tongued, patient, deeply knowledgeable about native flora. Carries grief and a secret.
characters.Marcel Dubois: The Collector. French orchid hunter. Aristocratic, charming, ruthless. Has ruined lives across three continents for rare specimens.

short_description: In colonial India, rival orchid hunters race through Assam for a legendary flower that demands sacrifice.
